testimonial_1_text: I came to Poland from Hungary with a Hungarian residence card. Everything about legalization was unclear to me. Fortunately, I found Sadykov Group — they took care of all the paperwork and guided me through the entire process. Now everything is clear, and my legalization process is underway. I am very grateful!
testimonial_2_text: I submitted my residence card application in Poland through Sadykov Group — I quickly received a stamp in my passport, and I'm now confident I'll get a decision and the card soon. Thank you to the whole team for your professionalism and quick assistance!
testimonial_3_text: Hello, my name is Tavrat Behkovjan. I arrived from Slovakia seven months ago. I applied for legalization through the Sadykov group and found a job. I found them in a Telegram group. Today I submitted my fingerprints and they issued me a red stamp. Thank you very much!

faq_1_q: How can a citizen of Uzbekistan or Tajikistan find a job in Poland?
faq_1_a: Contact our agency — we will find a vacancy, help with documents, and arrange an official contract. On average the process takes 1 to 3 days
faq_2_q: Do you assist with residence legalization in Poland?
faq_2_a: Yes, we provide full support at every stage of legalization: residence card, work permit, and interaction with Polish government authorities
faq_3_q: What documents are needed to work in Poland?
faq_3_a: Usually a passport is enough. We help arrange the remaining documents on-site — we will explain everything in detail during a free consultation
faq_4_q: Is it possible to work in Poland without knowing Polish?
faq_4_a: Yes, most of our job offers do not require knowledge of the Polish language. We specifically select positions with this in mind
faq_5_q: How much does employment through your agency cost?
faq_5_a: Employment requires no upfront payment. A fee is only possible after you start working
partners_desc: We work with trusted Polish employers across the country
